   "created_at": "2022-01-24T15:12:39Z",
   "github_updated_at": "2022-01-25T08:45:57Z",
   "body_snippet": "I do not know how rdp works so this is basically a question about whether it is possible.\r\n\r\nWhen I enable clipboard and connect to the remote server, server's clipboard is automatically updated with my local computer's clipboard.\r\nIs there anyway to prevent this or can this be implemented?\r\n\r\nThe scenario is this: we are running a software that is using clipboard. If I connect while the software is running and if I had something on my local computer's clipboard, server's clipboard is updated. T...",
-  "our_status": "new",
-  "priority": null,
+  "our_status": "triaged",
+  "priority": "P2-bug",
   "target_release": null,
   "our_branch": null,
   "our_pr": null,
-  "iterations": [],
+  "iterations": [
+    {
+      "seq": 1,
+      "date": "2026-02-23",
+      "type": "triaged",
+      "description": "The remote clipboard is updated prematurely, leading to unexpected behavior. It should only update when a paste action is initiated towards the remote session or a copy is made from it."
+    }
+  ],
   "comments": [
     {
       "id": "IC_kwDOAAcIMM480HaW",
@@ -79,5 +86,5 @@
   "needs_action": true,
   "waiting_for_us": true,
   "last_synced": "2026-02-15T09:52:50Z",
-  "notes": ""
+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): The remote clipboard is updated prematurely, leading to unexpected behavior. It should only update when a paste action is initiated towards the remote session or a copy is made from it.\nApproach: Modify the clipboard synchronization logic to prevent automatic remote clipboard updates on local clipboard changes. Instead, remote clipboard updates should be triggered only by explicit paste actions from local to remote, or copy actions from remote."
 }

diff --git a/run-tests.ps1 b/run-tests.ps1
index aef23b233..1ff58b269 100644
--- a/run-tests.ps1
+++ b/run-tests.ps1
@@ -36,24 +36,44 @@ if (-not (Test-Path $backupDir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $backupDir
 Copy-Item $testDll "$backupDir\mRemoteNGTests.dll" -Force
 
 function Parse-TestOutput($out) {
-    $p = 0; $f = 0; $s = 0
-    if ($out -match 'Passed\s*[:\s]+(\d+)')  { $p = [int]$Matches[1] }
-    if ($out -match 'Failed\s*[:\s]+(\d+)')  { $f = [int]$Matches[1] }
-    if ($out -match 'Skipped\s*[:\s]+(\d+)') { $s = [int]$Matches[1] }
-    $crashed = (($out -match "crashed") -or ($out -match "aborted")) -and (($p + $f) -eq 0)
-    return @{ Passed=$p; Failed=$f; Skipped=$s; Crashed=$crashed }
+    $p = 0; $f = 0; $s = 0; $total = 0
+    # Extract summary from last 30 lines to avoid matching per-test "Passed" lines
+    $allLines = $out -split "`n"
+    $tail = ($allLines | Select-Object -Last 30) -join "`n"
+    # dotnet test normal verbosity summary format:
+    #   Total tests: 2815
+    #        Passed: 2815
+    if ($tail -match 'Total tests:\s+(\d+)') { $total = [int]$Matches[1] }
+    if ($tail -match '^\s+Passed\s*:\s+(\d+)') { $p = [int]$Matches[1] }
+    if ($tail -match '^\s+Failed\s*:\s+(\d+)') { $f = [int]$Matches[1] }
+    if ($tail -match '^\s+Skipped\s*:\s+(\d+)') { $s = [int]$Matches[1] }
+    # Fallback: minimal verbosity format (Passed: N, Failed: N)
+    if ($p -eq 0 -and $tail -match 'Passed:\s+(\d+),') { $p = [int]$Matches[1] }
+    if ($f -eq 0 -and $tail -match 'Failed:\s+(\d+),') { $f = [int]$Matches[1] }
+    $crashed = (($out -match "crashed") -or ($out -match "aborted")) -and ($total -eq 0) -and ($p -eq 0)
+    return @{ Passed=$p; Failed=$f; Skipped=$s; Total=$total; Crashed=$crashed }
 }
 
+# Kill stale test processes
+Get-Process testhost -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Stop-Process -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+Get-Process testhost.x86 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Stop-Process -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+
 $totalPassed = 0; $totalFailed = 0; $totalSkipped = 0; $anyCrashed = $false
 $startTime = Get-Date
 
 # Phase 1: ALL tests EXCEPT FrmOptions (single process)
 Write-Host "`n[Phase 1] All tests (except FrmOptions)..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
 $mainFilter = "FullyQualifiedName!~OptionsFormTests&FullyQualifiedName!~AllOptionsPagesTests"
-$out1 = & dotnet test $testDll --filter $mainFilter --verbosity minimal 2>&1 | Out-String
+$out1 = & dotnet test $testDll --filter $mainFilter --verbosity normal 2>&1 | Out-String
 $r1 = Parse-TestOutput $out1
 Write-Host "  Passed: $($r1.Passed), Failed: $($r1.Failed), Skipped: $($r1.Skipped)" -ForegroundColor $(if ($r1.Failed -gt 0 -or $r1.Crashed) { "Red" } else { "Green" })
 if ($r1.Crashed) { Write-Host "  CRASH DETECTED" -ForegroundColor Red; $anyCrashed = $true }
+# Minimum test count guard — detect phantom/partial runs
+$MIN_PHASE1_TESTS = 2500
+if ($r1.Passed -lt $MIN_PHASE1_TESTS -and -not $r1.Crashed) {
+    Write-Host "  WARNING: Only $($r1.Passed) tests detected (expected >$MIN_PHASE1_TESTS). Possible silent crash." -ForegroundColor Red
+    $anyCrashed = $true
+}
 $totalPassed += $r1.Passed; $totalFailed += $r1.Failed; $totalSkipped += $r1.Skipped
 
 # Phase 2: FrmOptions isolated (each test in its own process)
@@ -65,7 +85,7 @@ $isolatedFilters = @(
 
 foreach ($t in $isolatedFilters) {
     Write-Host "  [$($t.Name)] " -NoNewline
-    $out = & dotnet test $testDll --filter $t.Filter --verbosity minimal 2>&1 | Out-String
+    $out = & dotnet test $testDll --filter $t.Filter --verbosity normal 2>&1 | Out-String
     $r = Parse-TestOutput $out
     if ($r.Crashed) {
         Write-Host "CRASHED" -ForegroundColor Red; $anyCrashed = $true
@@ -80,7 +100,7 @@ foreach ($t in $isolatedFilters) {
 # Phase 3: Specs (skip in headless mode)
 if (-not $Headless -and (Test-Path $specsDll)) {
     Write-Host "`n[Phase 3] Specs (FlaUI)..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
-    $out = & dotnet test $specsDll --verbosity minimal 2>&1 | Out-String
+    $out = & dotnet test $specsDll --verbosity normal 2>&1 | Out-String
     $r = Parse-TestOutput $out
     $specColor = if ($r.Failed -gt 0) { "Yellow" } else { "Green" }
     Write-Host "  Specs: $($r.Passed)p/$($r.Failed)f" -ForegroundColor $specColor
